name: Jacob's Cattle
image_alt: White beans mottled with deep maroon markings
description: A striking heirloom dry bean with white seeds heavily mottled with maroon-burgundy markings — each bean is unique. Earthy, rich flavor with a creamy texture when cooked. Excellent drought tolerance.
history: Brought by the Passamaquoddy people of Maine to European settlers, making it one of the oldest beans in continuous cultivation in North America. The name derives from the biblical Jacob's speckled cattle.

good.reason: Compatible root depths, fixes nitrogen for nearby plants

seasonal_quirks: Extremely drought-tolerant once established — ideal for dry climates and seasons. Needs full season to mature for dry bean use. Can be harvested green as a shell bean for a shorter season option.
harvest_cues: For dry beans: pods should be fully papery and rattling when shaken. For shell beans: pods plump and beginning to show color but not yet papery.
variety_notes: The mottled markings fade somewhat when cooked but remain visible. Store dried beans in an airtight container away from light — the beautiful markings last for months.
preservation: Dry fully and store in airtight containers for 2+ years. Excellent canned for long-term storage.
